metadata.title: Oracle AI Agent Studio - Use Azure Foundry LLM (External LLM Setup)
metadata.source: Oracle Fusion AI Docs + Oracle FusionInsider Blog

overview: Oracle AI Agent Studio (built into Oracle Fusion Applications) supports bringing your own LLM. Azure Foundry models are added via the 'OpenAI via Microsoft Azure' provider option under the Credentials tab.
supported_external_providers: OpenAI (via Microsoft Azure) — includes Azure Foundry deployed models

quickstart.title: Add Azure Foundry LLM to Oracle Fusion Agent Studio

steps.where: Oracle Fusion Apps → AI Agent Studio
steps.navigation: Go to AI Agent Studio > Credentials tab

steps.action: Select your LLM when building an agent
steps.where: Agent Studio > New Agent / Edit Agent > LLM selection
sub_steps: In the LLM dropdown, select your saved Azure credential
Use built-in test panel to verify LLM responds
Deploy agent to your Fusion workflow

key_facts.philosophy: Built-in, not bolted-on — Agent Studio is native to Oracle Fusion Apps
key_facts.no_code_option: Can create agents with no code via Agent Studio UI
key_facts.oracle_default_llms: Oracle provides its own optimized LLMs for Fusion Apps by default
key_facts.custom_llm: You can replace or supplement Oracle LLMs with any external LLM
key_facts.mcp_support: MCP (Model Context Protocol) supported for external tool integration
key_facts.marketplace: Oracle AI Agent Studio has a marketplace for pre-built agents/tools

azure_foundry_specific.why: Azure Foundry serves OpenAI-compatible models (GPT-4, DeepSeek, etc.) via Azure OpenAI endpoints — same API format Oracle supports

credential_fields.endpoint: Your Azure Foundry project endpoint URL
credential_fields.api_key: Azure API key from Keys & Endpoint section
credential_fields.deployment_name: The name you gave your model deployment

compatible_models: Any OpenAI-compatible model deployed on Azure Foundry

troubleshooting.solution: Ensure endpoint URL includes trailing slash and is HTTPS
Check API key is valid and deployment is active in Azure Foundry portal
Credentials must be saved first before appearing in agent LLM selection
Ensure your Oracle Fusion user has AI Agent Studio admin role

summary: Azure Foundry LLMs plug into Oracle AI Agent Studio via the 'OpenAI via Microsoft Azure' credential provider. Takes about 5 minutes. Go to Agent Studio > Credentials > Add > OpenAI (via Microsoft Azure) > paste your Azure Foundry endpoint + API key + deployment name. Done.
